utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 corresponding items. Now check if each is fully specified with all details as required: each item must include coverage type and costs. In the plan, each item has a description and a cost. Health_coverage: specifies type (physical and mental health) and cost (€950/month). Property_coverage: specifies home/contents insurance and cost (€95/month). Travel_coverage: specifies travel insurance and cost (€230/month). Additional_services: includes services and indicates cost as 'Included in other premiums' (cost is provided, albeit bundled). Total_budget: provides overall monthly cost (€1,275/month). All items are present and include costs, satisfying the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We need to map each item in the final coverage plan to an option in the ground-truth ratings. The plan lists: (1) health_coverage described as a Corporate Benefits Package for global executive physical health and family mental health. The ground truth lists specific plan names for health (EliteCare Premium Plan, PrimeCare Premium Plan, etc.), but 'Corporate Benefits Package' is not among them. Therefore, there is no exact match to map and we should not invent a rating. (2) property_coverage explicitly names 'EliteHome Premium Coverage,' which exists in property_protection.home_content with rating 10. (3) travel_coverage explicitly names 'PrimeTravel Premium Plan,' which exists in mobility_coverage.travel_insurance with rating 10. (4) additional_services mentions 'executive liability protection, golf/recreation coverage' and concierge claim service, but these are not plan names in the ground-truth list. (5) total_budget is not an insurance product. Therefore, only two items can be mapped to exact ground-truth entries: EliteHome Premium Coverage and PrimeTravel Premium Plan.
